name: Life's inequalities.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Justice and injustice.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Small trespasses punished; large crimes condoned.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Ass punished for stealing mouthful of grass; lion and wolf forgiven for eating sheep.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Animals confess sins to one another: fox and wolf forgive each other; punish ass. (Cf. V20.)
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Animals confess sins to lion holding court. All the powerful animals forgiven. Ass and lamb are punished.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
additional_description: All the powerful animals forgiven. Ass and lamb are punished.

name: Penitent in confession worries about little sins and belittles the big ones.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
bibliographies: *Wesselski Arlotto I 191 No. 17; Italian Novella: Rotunda.

name: He who steals much called king; he who steals little called robber.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
bibliographies: *Pauli (ed. Bolte) No. 351; Scala Celi 20a No. 120; Alphabet No. 334; Spanish Exempla: Keller.

name: Wolf punished for theft; kings honored.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Largest burdens laid on smallest asses: best offices to most ignorant men.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Unworthy rewarded instead of the worthy.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Fool laughs at the absurdities he sees about him. (1) Sees a man who is to die that day buy shoes. (2) Sees sheriff leading a man to the gallows: a big thief leading a little one. (3) Sees farmer weeping at funeral of his child, while priest (the real father) sings.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
additional_description: (1) Sees a man who is to die that day buy shoes. (2) Sees sheriff leading a man to the gallows: a big thief leading a little one. (3) Sees farmer weeping at funeral of his child, while priest (the real father) sings.

name: Dwarf king (fairy) laughs at the absurdities he sees about him. (1) Sees man who is to die that day complain that his shoes are too thin. (Cf. J2174.) (2) Sees women adorn their heads when they are immoral below. (Cf. J2050.) (3) Sees man remove from his wife's garment dust gathered while she lay with another man. (Cf. J2301.3.) (4) Sees persons making plans while forgetting to say, "If God wills."
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
additional_description: (1) Sees man who is to die that day complain that his shoes are too thin.

name: Philosopher laughs at the vanities and sins of the world. His companion weeps.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: The fathers have eaten sour grapes and the children's teeth are set on edge.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Justice depends on the point of view.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Hen complains that man eats her, but she eats ant.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: A gift of property silences criticism. A priest who has preached against lucrative places given the clergy is silent when he receives one.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
additional_description: A priest who has preached against lucrative places given the clergy is silent when he receives one.

name: Man complains of injustice of gods' wrecking ship because of one man's sin. He then kills whole swarm of ants because one has stung him.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 77 (ET 429), 103 (ST 161); Halm No. 118; Irish: O'Suilleabhain 11, Beal XXI 305.
additional_description: He then kills whole swarm of ants because one has stung him.

name: Wolf objects to lion stealing sheep from him although he has himself stolen it.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Judge reduces penalty when accused is his own son. [Inadvertant duplication of J1197.]
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: GANGES VALLEY . Shyama- Shankar 178 .

name: Theft to avoid starvation forgiven.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Swallow killed in court house by snake laments injustice in house of justice.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Rights of the strong.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Wolf unjustly accuses lamb and eats him. When all the lamb's defenses are good the wolf asserts the right of the strong over the weak. (Usually accused of stirring up water from lower in stream.) (Cf. U141.)
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 50 (ET 97), 148 (ST 526); Halm Aesop No. 274; *Crane Vitry 191 No. 135; Herbert III 12; Alphabet No. 631; Jacobs Aesop 199 No. 2; Italian Novella: Rotunda; Indonesia: DeVries's list No. 83 (monkey and goat).
additional_description: When all the lamb's defenses are good the wolf asserts the right of the strong over the weak. (Usually accused of stirring up water from lower in stream.)

name: Cat unjustly accuses cock and eats him. Although all the cock's defenses are good the cat tells him that she can no longer go hungry and eats him.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
additional_description: Although all the cock's defenses are good the cat tells him that she can no longer go hungry and eats him.

name: Crow exercises rights of strong over dove who cannot defend self. Dove at crow's request sings to save her brood which crow kills after song.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
additional_description: Dove at crow's request sings to save her brood which crow kills after song.

name: Smallness of offense no excuse when hunter prepares to kill lark. She has stolen but a single grain of wheat.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 64 (ET 273), 146 (ST 513); Halm Aesop No. 209.
additional_description: She has stolen but a single grain of wheat.

name: Cock killed by his captors in spite of his plea of usefulness to man.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Nobleman murders one of the people. Goes unpunished.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Nobleman who kills one of the people is brought to justice only after long delay.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Rich man (king) seizes poor widow's (nun's) cow.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
bibliographies: Herbert III 40ff. No. 39; Hervieux IV No. 40; Irish myth: *Cross.

name: King seizes old woman's cow.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: King seizes poor man's lamb.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Nobles ruin peasant's (widow's) crops with impunity.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: GANGES VALLEY . Grier- son Survey IX ( 1 ) 421f . - BOMBAY . Jethabhai 93-5 No. 34 .

name: Lion cub killed by bull gives lioness no right to complain.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 50 (ET 92), 129 (ST 367); Halm Aesop No. 395.

name: Wolf as commander orders all booty divided, but keeps his own.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 47 (ET 58), 103 (ST 157); Halm Aesop No. 281.

name: Ruler exiles subject for trivial remark.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Ruler forces bishop to ordain ignorant priest.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Praise what your master likes and scorn what he dislikes.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Wealth and poverty.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Dividing after God's fashion: little to poor, much to rich.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
bibliographies: *Wesselski Hodscha Nasreddin II 233 No. 509, Bebel I 232 No. 149, Mensa Philosophica No. 63.

name: Priest has no friends until he becomes bishop: then they flock to him.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Wealth is relative: beggar with horse, wife, or dog considered rich by poorer beggar.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Grass serves as pleasant couch for poor.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Every man has his price.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Every woman has her price.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Bid raised for queen's favors until she hesitates. Jester replies, "If a man have goods enough, he might have a sovereign lady."
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
additional_description: Jester replies, "If a man have goods enough, he might have a sovereign lady."

name: Jester takes cow and tells king people have plenty of milk, for "he who is warm thinks everyone else is."
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Optimist becomes pessimist when his money is stolen.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: INDIA . Venkataswami 127 . -BOMBAY . Marathi : Manwaring 72 .

name: Man so constantly enriched by God that he cannot give all his goods to the poor.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Great possessions bring great risks.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Dove's pride in her large brood linked with fear for their loss.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 44 (ET 21), 141 (ST 470); Halm Aesop No. 358.

name: King in exile neglected, but courted when he regains throne.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Price of object depends on where it is on sale.
division1: U0–U99. Life's inequalities.

name: Appearances deceive.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Many books do not make a scholar.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Many books do not make a scholar: youth tricks mother by carrying many books.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Beard on she-goats do not make a male.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Big voice: little creature. (Frogs, crickets.)
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 61, *66 (ET 232, 304), 123 (ST 321, 322); Halm Aesop Nos. 172, 248.

name: Mountain in labor brings forth a mouse.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI *44 (ET 24), *101 (ST 141); Jacobs Aesop 203 No. 14; Spanish Exempla: Keller.

name: The skeleton in the closet. An apparently happy man lets another see the actual misery of his existence.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
additional_description: An apparently happy man lets another see the actual misery of his existence.

name: Apparently happy woman discloses the skeleton of her slain paramour.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Hypocrite is acclaimed as saint after his death.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Man rejects bride because she seems immature. Her father reassures him that she has had several children already.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
additional_description: Her father reassures him that she has had several children already.

name: Other ways in which appearances deceive.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Revelations of a satyr.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: At a funeral of a child a satyr reveals that the real father (officiating priest) sings while the cuckold weeps. (Cf. U15.)
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: At a hanging the witnesses are bigger thieves than the culprit. (Cf. U10.)
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Peasant wishes to exchange places with monks. Thinks they lead a very easy life. One night's experience in the monastery makes him change his mind.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
additional_description: Thinks they lead a very easy life. One night's experience in the monastery makes him change his mind.

name: Handsome exterior does not indicate beautiful soul. Angel holds nose when handsome sinner passes.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
additional_description: Angel holds nose when handsome sinner passes.

name: An ugly face does not mean an ugly soul. A noble and holy man who is very ugly is proved to be a saintly soul.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
additional_description: A noble and holy man who is very ugly is proved to be a saintly soul.

name: Stories to show that one's name does not alter his condition.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: BOMBAY . Mara- thi : Manwaring 136 .

name: Nature will show itself.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Like parent, like child.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Crab walks backward: learned from his parents.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: *Crane Vitry 152 No. 44; Wienert FFC LVI *63 (ET 258), *103 (ST 159); Halm Aesop No. 187.

name: Hind, like his mother, flees before the hounds. Useless for her to urge him to stand up against them.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI *63 (ET 256), 103 (ST 158, 501); Halm Aesop No. 130.
additional_description: Useless for her to urge him to stand up against them.

name: Farmer's son and noble's reared in country. The former takes to toil on farm, the latter to riding and hunting.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
additional_description: The former takes to toil on farm, the latter to riding and hunting.

name: Alleged son of king proved to be bastard when he displays habits of his true father.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Father, like son, acts foolishly at king's court.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: BOMBAY . Jethab- hai 165f . No. 66 .

name: Butcher's son becomes cruel and atrocious ruler.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: GANGES VALLEY . Hindu- stani : Chilli 159f . No. 11 .

name: Dungbeetle prefers his dunghill to all other smells.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 60 (ET 217), 119 (ST 292); Halm Aesop No. 185; Herbert III 35ff.; India: Thompson-Balys: PUNJAB . Swynnerton Raja Rasalu 179 .

name: Beetles treated with as much honor as oxen will not learn to act as oxen.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Hog goes to bath but wallows in the mud.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Scorpion, in spite of himself, stings the turtle carrying him across the stream. Is drowned.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Wolf loses interest in the sermon when he sees a flock of sheep. A dervish preaches to him.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: Chauvin II 125 No. 123; Scala Celi 50b No. 285; Spanish Exempla: Keller.

name: Heron (crane) loses interest in heaven if there are no snails.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: MADRAS . Ta- mil : Robinson 40 ; Telugu : Pantulu Telugus 83 .

name: Dog allowed to warm self in house begins to bark. Is chased off.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Fawn, in spite of his fine horns, runs from the dog.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 63 (ET 257), 89 (ST 12): Halm Aesop No. 303.

name: Tiger son of human mother scratches her and licks her blood.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: ASSAM HILLS . Sema Naga : Hutton 317 .

name: Tiger father of human child licks its blood.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: ASSAM HILLS . Lhota Naga : Mills 190 .

name: Nature will show itself – miscellaneous.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Thieving nature of the fox will show itself.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Prostitute will deceive new lover as always.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: CENT . INDIA . Balag- hat : Elwin ( 1944 ) 312 .

name: Washerman as minister thinks of washing and fails the king. [Inadvertant duplication of J677.]
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: MADRAS . Telugu : Pantulu Telugus 22 .

name: The power of habit.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Newly rich cannot resist call for distribution of food to beggars.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: BOMBAY . Jet- habhai 130ff . No. 50 .

name: Familiarity takes away fear.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Fox finally converses with lion whom he had feared at first.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 60 (ET 224), 111 (ST 224); Halm Aesop No. 39; Jacobs Aesop 208 No. 34.

name: Men at first frightened at camel take him into their service.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 66 (ET 306), 111 (ST 224); Halm Aesop No. 180.

name: Man soon learns to stand the smells of the tannery.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 81 (ET 474), 111 (ST 226); Halm Aesop No. 368; India: Thompson-Balys: GANGES VALLEY . Shyama- Shankar 17 2f .

name: Farmer prefers stable smells to flowers. Latter make him ill.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: Wesselski Mönchslatein 13 No. 8; Spanish Exempla: Keller.

name: Knight doesn't want to go to heaven if there are no hunting dogs there.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Longing for accustomed food and living.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: At feast cat chooses rat meat; other animals cannot eat it.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Shepherd in king's palace sickens for country air.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: BOMBAY . Jethabhai 182ff . No. 73.- MYSORE . Frere Deccan 162f .

name: Former slave sickens for accustomed food.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: Spanish Exempla: Keller; India: Thompson-Balys: PUNJAB . Bannu : Thorburn 178

name: Peasant girl married to king longs for peasant ways of eating.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Peasants fed white bread demand the rye bread to which they are accustomed.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Fisher and hunter exchange catches for variety: soon return to original food.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Dissatisfied workmen exchange work: still more dissatisfied.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: Bødker Exempler 276 No. 14; India: Thompson-Balys: BENGAL . Day 152ff . No. 11 ( 1 ) .

name: Beasts and fishes exchange places: fatal to both.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: INDIA . Raju Fa- bles 35f .

name: Mill horse when taken to war keeps going in a circle, as he has learned in the mill.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Habit of dishonesty (thievery) cannot be broken.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Dishonest silversmith restless until silver ornament is mixed with alloy.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Thieves cannot quit plundering.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Power of habit – miscellaneous.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Habitual food and drink continued even when it is harmful.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Conservatism defies reason.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Conservative but absurd way of slaughtering hog stubbornly kept.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: GANGES VALLEY . Shyama - Shankar 158

name: One man's food is another man's poison.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U140. One man's food is another man's poison.

name: Enmity between fisherman and dweller on the river. Fishing stirs up the water and makes it unfit for drinking. (Cf. U31.)
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U140. One man's food is another man's poison.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 84 (ET 512), 121 (ST 303); Halm Aesop No. 25.
additional_description: Fishing stirs up the water and makes it unfit for drinking.

name: Ox likes loving strokes of man; flea fears them.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U140. One man's food is another man's poison.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 60 (ET 223), 121 (ST 307); Halm Aesop No. 426.

name: Collier and fuller cannot live together: one makes things clean, the other soils them.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U140. One man's food is another man's poison.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI 85 (ET 513), 121 (ST 305); Halm Aesop No. 59.

name: Nightingale cannot lodge with birds whose nest is made of manure.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U140. One man's food is another man's poison.

name: Royalty unable to endure coarse entertainment.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U140. One man's food is another man's poison.

name: Coarse food, etc., sends adulterous king back to his wife.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U140. One man's food is another man's poison.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: PUNJAB . Temple Legends III 226 .

name: Animals try unsuccessfully to exchange food.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U140. One man's food is another man's poison.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: U.P . Oudh : Crooke - Rouse 177f . No. 40. - CEYLON . Parker II 440 .

name: Good weather for one is foul for another.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U140. One man's food is another man's poison.
bibliographies: Cf. Heywood's Play of the Weather.

name: Bird wants sunshine, worm clouds.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U140. One man's food is another man's poison.

name: What is one man's food is another man's poison – miscellaneous.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U140. One man's food is another man's poison.

name: Lions despise what asses admire (braying).
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U140. One man's food is another man's poison.

name: Indifference of the miserable.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Ass indifferent to enemy's approach: he could be no more miserable than now.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Misfortune with oneself to blame the hardest.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U160. Misfortune with oneself to blame the hardest.

name: Eagle killed with arrow made with his own feather.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U160. Misfortune with oneself to blame the hardest.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LVI *64 (ET 277), 117 (ST 273); Halm Aesop No. 4; Spanish Exempla: Keller.

name: Tree cut down with axe for which it has furnished a handle.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U160. Misfortune with oneself to blame the hardest.
bibliographies: Wienert FFC LXI *74 (ET 388), 117 (ST 274); Halm Aesop No. 123; Jewish: *Neuman; India: Thompson-Balys: INDIA . Raju Fables 47f ; Venkataswami 125. - BOMBAY . Jethabhai 152 No. 60 .

name: Behavior of the blind.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Blind man crosses a narrow bridge which his guide is afraid to attempt.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Two blind men succeed in fooling each other about their blindness.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: PUNJAB . Swynnerton Upper Indus 271f .

name: Futile attempt to explain to a blind man meaning of "white".
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: In vino veritas.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Man unable to persuade wife to confess misdeed to priest succeeds when he makes her drunk. (Cf. J1141.)
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Bad ruler, bad subject.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: No great knights now because no great kings.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: To have good servants a lord must be good.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Forced peace valueless.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Monk under pressure from abbot forgives the crucifix which has fallen and hurt him. He says that nevertheless there will always be hatred between them.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
additional_description: He says that nevertheless there will always be hatred between them.

name: The nature of sin.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Monk leaves monastery when he sees a devil there tempting brethren. Returns when in the world he sees scores of devils tempting the people.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
additional_description: Returns when in the world he sees scores of devils tempting the people.

name: Hermit having rebuked youth falls himself when exposed to the same sin.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Monk rebukes brethren who succumb to temptation. When he is exposed to it he understands what temptation is.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
additional_description: When he is exposed to it he understands what temptation is.

name: No place secret enough for sin.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: Alphabet No. 3; Wienert FFC LVI 80 (ET 458), 138 (ST 441); Halm No. 354.

name: Lying is incurable. A father asks about his son. When he hears that he lies, he gives the son up as hopeless. Other sins may be outgrown.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: Pauli (ed. Bolte) No. 393; Spanish Exempla: Keller.
additional_description: A father asks about his son. When he hears that he lies, he gives the son up as hopeless. Other sins may be outgrown.

name: Liar cannot be healed even when taking bath in the Ganges.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: False repentance of the sick. Wolf having eaten too much meat promises God to eat no more meat. When he becomes well he eats as before.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
additional_description: Wolf having eaten too much meat promises God to eat no more meat. When he becomes well he eats as before.

name: False repentance of the sick, rich man. After his death it was discovered that he had intended to retrieve his money from church if he recovered.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
additional_description: After his death it was discovered that he had intended to retrieve his money from church if he recovered.

name: Power of mind over body.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U240. Power of mind over body.

name: King grows lean from fear of death.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U240. Power of mind over body.

name: Hares fearing death outrun pursuing dogs.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U240. Power of mind over body.

name: The hare's last will. Surrounded by hunters and hounds, the hare considers to whom each of his members will be allotted. Succeeds in escaping.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U240. Power of mind over body.
bibliographies: Lithuanian: Balys Index No. 160*; Prussian: Plenzat 8.
additional_description: Surrounded by hunters and hounds, the hare considers to whom each of his members will be allotted. Succeeds in escaping.

name: Courage conquers all and impossible is made possible.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
section: U240. Power of mind over body.
bibliographies: India: Thompson-Balys: KASHMIR . Jammu : Brown Tawi MS No. 42 .

name: Shortness of life.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: The bad custom in the world: the young die as well as the old. Hence youth enters monastery.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
bibliographies: *Crane Vitry 184 No. 116; Alphabet No. 212; *Mensa Philosophica No. 181.

name: Passage of time.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Time seems short to those who play, long for those who wait. So says servant girl whose mistress upbraids her for late hours.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.
additional_description: So says servant girl whose mistress upbraids her for late hours.

name: Suffering healed by time.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Security breeds indifference.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Cat ceases catching rats as soon as he is given a home in a monastery. Thus with lazy priests.
division1: U100–U299. The nature of life – miscellaneous motifs.

name: Worship of the Pleiades
Sacred valley
Sacred isle

Dead buried with feet toward sun

Word of God (Christian) comes down in lightning flashes
